description: Complete the Rust port of Talon (Obsidian vault search engine) from where Phase 4 ended. Phases 0-4 (workspace, store, search algorithms, indexer/sync, embed pipeline, sqlite-vec) are landed with 206 passing tests. Remaining work: query handlers (search/read/related/meta/changes/lint/status), MCP server, filesystem watcher, TS wrapper with per-platform binaries, parity tests, and final polish. Reference TS at /tmp/talon-scaffold-imports/ultra/edge/src/services/talon/. Standing rules: never silence clippy lints with #[allow] (real fixes only); use wiremock for sidecar HTTP tests; real rusqlite Connection for DB tests (no mocks); pin deps to full semver; project conventions are eyre/color-eyre, time (not chrono), fs-err (not std::fs), serde_yaml_ng, blocking rusqlite + reqwest::blocking + spawn_blocking.

userStories.title: Add LLM expansion HTTP client
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a blocking OpenAI-compatible chat client so the hybrid search pipeline can request query expansion variants from the local LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/expansion/{mod,client,types,error}.rs
Public surface: ExpansionClient::new(base_url, model) and expand(query: &str, n_variants: u8) -> Result<Vec<String>, ExpansionError>
POSTs /chat/completions with system prompt ported from search/hybrid-expand.ts
Returns empty Vec on malformed JSON (never panics); HTTP errors map to ExpansionError
PII redaction reuses inference::redact
Wiremock tests: happy path, malformed-JSON fallback, HTTP 5xx
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/expansion/{mod,client,types,error}.rs. ExpansionClient::new(base_url, model) + expand(query, n_variants) -> Result<Vec<String>, ExpansionError>. Posts /chat/completions with system prompt from local-llm.ts. Malformed JSON returns Ok(vec![]). HTTP errors map to ExpansionError::Http. PII redaction reuses inference::redact. Wiremock tests cover happy path, malformed-JSON fallback, and HTTP 5xx. 212 tests pass.

userStories.title: Add persistent llm_cache with TTL
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a SQL-backed cache for LLM expansion responses so repeated queries skip the LLM round trip across process restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/expansion/cache.rs
Public surface: LlmCache::new(conn), get(key), put(key, value, ttl_ms), purge_expired()
Uses existing llm_cache table from migrations (key, value, expires_at_ms columns)
Tests: round-trip, expiry past TTL returns None, purge_expired removes stale rows
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/expansion/cache.rs: LlmCache<'conn> with new(conn), get(key), put(key, value, ttl_ms), purge_expired(). Updated llm_cache migration to (key, value, expires_at_ms) schema. Exported from expansion::mod and lib.rs. 5 tests (round-trip, missing-key, expired-entry, overwrite, purge). 217 tests pass.

userStories.title: Compose hybrid-single retrieval
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a single-query retrieval function that runs bm25 + fuzzy_title + vector in parallel and returns the three result lists for downstream RRF fusion.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/hybrid_single.rs
Public surface: run_hybrid_single(conn, query, embedding: Option<&[f32]>, limit) -> HybridSingleResult { bm25, fuzzy_title_parts: TitleSearchParts, vector }
Composes existing search::bm25::search_bm25, search::fuzzy_title::search_title_parts, search::vector::search_vector — no new algorithm code
Tests against seeded fixture: each retriever's contribution is isolated and complete
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/search/hybrid_single.rs. HybridSingleResult { bm25, fuzzy_title_parts: TitleSearchParts, vector }. run_hybrid_single(conn, query, embedding: Option<&[f32]>, limit) composes search_bm25, search_title_parts, search_vector. embedding=None skips vector call and returns empty bucket. Re-exported HybridSingleResult and run_hybrid_single from search::mod. 5 tests: bm25/fuzzy-title/vector bucket isolation, None-embedding guard, disjoint-note independence. 222 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build cross-encoder rerank pipeline
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a reranker that calls the sidecar /rerank endpoint and blends the cross-encoder scores into the hybrid scores using rank-tier weights.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/rerank_pipeline.rs
Public surface: rerank_candidates(inference, query, candidates: Vec<RawSearchResult>, top_k: u32) -> Vec<RawSearchResult>
Calls inference.rerank(query, texts, return_text=false), then composes existing fuse::blend_rerank_candidates
Honors RERANK_TOP_K from search/constants
Wiremock test for /rerank; blend math matches TS expectations within 1e-4
just verify passes
userStories.notes: Implemented talon-core/src/search/rerank_pipeline.rs. rerank_candidates(inference, query, candidates, top_k) calls inference.rerank with title+"\n\n"+snippet payload, composes fuse::blend_rerank_candidates. top_k caps candidates sent to reranker. Graceful degradation on HTTP error returns original candidates with hybrid scores unchanged. Added RERANK_TOP_K=40 to search/constants. 5 wiremock tests: happy path, blend math within 1e-4, HTTP 5xx fallback, empty-candidates early exit, top_k truncation. 227 tests pass.

userStories.title: Build hybrid-pipeline orchestrator
userStories.description: As Talon, I want the full hybrid search pipeline (probe, optional expansion, multi-variant retrieval, fuse, rerank, filter) wired together so the search command can call one function.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/search/hybrid_pipeline.rs
Public surface: run_hybrid_pipeline(conn, inference, expansion, query, options) -> Vec<RawSearchResult>
Strong-signal short-circuit via fuse::estimate_strong_signal — skips expansion+rerank when probe is decisive
Calls US-001 expansion + US-003 hybrid-single per variant + US-004 rerank
Wiremock end-to-end test: full pipeline against seeded fixture vault with mocked sidecar (chat + embed + rerank)
Strong-signal path verified: high-confidence query skips expansion call
just verify passes

userStories.title: Wire real search command
userStories.description: As a Talon CLI user, I want `talon search` to return real ranked results across hybrid/semantic/fulltext/title modes with scope-priority post-multiplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_search_stub with real handler calling US-005
Modes: hybrid (default), semantic (vector only), fulltext (bm25 only), title (fuzzy_title only)
Query embedding via blocking inference.embed for semantic/hybrid modes
Post-rerank: multiply each result.score by ScopePriority::multiplier(), then re-sort
--fast flag skips expansion + skips rerank
SearchResponse::empty_scaffold call sites removed
Integration test: end-to-end on temp vault (run_sync + run_embed_pass + search) with wiremock sidecar
just verify passes

userStories.title: Build read handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon read <path>` to return the note body with frontmatter stripped, optional line range, and a heading tree.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/read.rs with read_note(conn, vault_root, input: &ReadInput) -> ReadResponse
Supports --from-line / --max-lines slicing
Supports --raw mode (no frontmatter strip)
Returns headings tree extracted from body
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_read_stub
Tests: read fixture note with frontmatter, line range, raw mode, missing file → InvalidInput
just verify passes

userStories.title: Build related-notes handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon related <path>` to traverse the link graph in outgoing/backlinks/both directions up to a configured depth.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/related.rs with find_related(conn, input: &RelatedInput) -> RelatedResponse
Direction: Outgoing | Backlinks | Both; depth 1-3 (RELATED_DEFAULT_DEPTH=1)
Reuses existing links::compute_backlinks and resolve_wiki_links
Scope filter applied during traversal
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_related_stub
Tests: traversal on fixture graph (Graph/Parent → Graph/Child → Graph/Grandchild) with depth limits and direction variants
just verify passes

userStories.title: Build meta (frontmatter query) handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon meta` to filter notes by frontmatter --where clauses, project --select fields, aggregate --tag-counts, and resolve --sources reverse references.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/meta.rs with query_meta(conn, input: &MetaInput) -> MetaResponse
--where operators: =, !=, <, <=, >, >=, contains, exists (existing WhereClause/WhereOperator types)
--select projects requested frontmatter fields onto each entry
--tag-counts emits SELECT tag, COUNT(*) FROM note_tags GROUP BY tag
--sources resolves via ReverseSourceIndex
--since applies time-window filter
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_meta_stub
Tests: each operator on fixture vault, tag aggregation, source lookup
just verify passes

userStories.title: Build changes handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon changes --since <timestamp>` to return the added/modified/deleted notes from the event_log.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/changes.rs with get_changes(conn, since: Option<i64>) -> ChangesResponse
Returns { added, modified, deleted } populated from event_log table
--since parsing reuses change_tracking::parse_since
Replaces the bail!("changes is scaffolded but not implemented yet") in command.rs
Tests: simulate add → modify → delete sequence via direct DB writes; verify classification
just verify passes

userStories.title: Build lint handler
userStories.description: As an agent, I want `talon lint <check>` to surface graph health issues: orphans, broken-links, dangling-refs, unreferenced.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/query/lint.rs with run_lint(conn, check: LintCheck) -> LintResponse
Orphans: notes with zero incoming links
BrokenLinks: link target file does not exist
DanglingRefs: frontmatter reference to nonexistent path
Unreferenced: zero incoming AND zero outgoing
Replaces talon-cli/src/command.rs::emit_lint_stub
Tests: each check on fixture vault (Graph/Grandchild=orphan, Lifecycle/Doomed→nonexistent=broken)
just verify passes

userStories.title: Replace status stub with real index counts
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `talon status` to report real index health: active note count, chunk count, failed-embedding count, vector dimensions, scope report.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces StatusResponse::scaffold and command.rs::emit_status_stub
Real counts via SQL: COUNT(*) FROM notes WHERE active=1, COUNT(*) FROM chunks, COUNT(*) FROM chunks WHERE embedding_status='failed'
Vector dimensions via vec_ext::get_vec_chunks_dimensions
Scope report: configured scopes, default set, unscoped count
State: Ready when vault reachable + index exists; ConfigError when talon init not run
Tests: status against seeded vault, status with no DB returns ConfigError
just verify passes

userStories.title: Port fixture vault and add integration test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the 21-note fixture vault from the TS reference ported into a real on-disk fixture so integration tests exercise the full query layer end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Port 21 notes from /tmp/talon-scaffold-imports/.../tests/fixtures/talon/fixture-vault.ts to crates/talon-core/tests/fixtures/vault/ (Atlas/*, Graph/*, Search/*, Filters/*, Lifecycle/*)
New integration test crates/talon-core/tests/fixture_vault.rs
Runs run_sync, then exercises: search hybrid (mocked sidecar), search fulltext, related, meta-where, lint orphans, status
Asserts paths exact-match TS expectations from tests/services/talon/fixture-vault.integration.test.ts; scores within 1e-3
Suite green across 5 consecutive runs with no flake
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add MCP JSON-RPC stdio transport
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want `talon --mcp` to speak JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio so it can be embedded in MCP-aware agents.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-cli/src/mcp/{mod,transport,protocol}.rs
Choose between rmcp crate and hand-rolled JSON-RPC; document choice with reason in module docstring
Frame format: line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout (no Content-Length headers)
Methods supported: initialize, initialized notification, tools/list, tools/call, shutdown
Tests: handshake echo round-trips; malformed frame returns proper JSON-RPC error envelope
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add MCP tool schema and handler dispatch
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a single `talon` tool whose action-discriminated input dispatches to the same query handlers the CLI uses.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-cli/src/mcp/tool.rs
Tool name: talon (per design spec §11)
Input schema generated for each TalonInput variant (schemars crate or hand-written constants in tool.rs)
tools/call dispatches through the same handlers as US-006 through US-012; wraps TalonResponse as MCP CallToolResult
TalonError mapped to MCP error envelope with code + message
Tests: tools/list returns valid schema; each action's tools/call round-trips against the integration vault
just verify passes

userStories.title: Wire --mcp flag to long-running server
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `talon --mcp` to run as a long-lived process that handles requests until shutdown or SIGINT.
acceptanceCriteria: Replaces bail!("mcp mode is scaffolded but not implemented yet") in command.rs
Long-running loop with graceful shutdown on JSON-RPC shutdown method or SIGINT
Integration test: spawn `talon --mcp` subprocess, send tools/list + tools/call, verify response and clean exit
Manual smoke: `talon --mcp <<< '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{}}'` returns valid response
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add filesystem watcher event source
userStories.description: As Talon, I want a debounced fs-event source that coalesces per-path Created/Modified/Deleted/Renamed events so the watcher does not thrash on save bursts.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/watcher/{mod,events,debounce}.rs
New deps: notify and notify-debouncer-mini, pinned to full semver
Public surface: WatcherHandle::new(vault_root, debounce_ms) -> (handle, mpsc::Receiver<WatchEvent>)
Coalesces per-path events; emits Created/Modified/Deleted/Renamed after the debounce window
Tests: temp dir with file ops verifies coalesced events (single create→modify→modify yields one Modified)
just verify passes

userStories.title: Wire watcher events to indexer
userStories.description: As Talon, I want watcher events to drive incremental sync via index_one_note and perform_note_deletion so the index stays current without a full scan.
acceptanceCriteria: New module talon-core/src/watcher/dispatch.rs
Created/Modified → index_one_note(conn, path, …)
Deleted/Renamed-from → perform_note_deletion(conn, path)
Acquires existing sync lock briefly per batch, releases immediately
Drops events for paths matching indexer::matches_ignore_patterns
Tests: temp vault, write/edit/delete a file, watcher dispatches and DB reflects state
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add talon watch CLI command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `talon watch` to be a long-running command that incrementally syncs the vault on every fs change, with --once for tests.
acceptanceCriteria: New "watch" => emit_watch(args, rest).await arm in command.rs
Loads config, registers sqlite-vec, opens DB, spawns watcher
Long-running; SIGINT triggers clean shutdown that drains pending events and releases lock
--once flag: drain pending events then exit (tests/CI use this)
Integration test: spawn `talon watch --once` against temp vault, verify exit + DB state
talon watch --help renders
just verify passes

userStories.title: Scaffold ts/ npm package with binary resolver
userStories.description: As a Node consumer, I want a `@talon/cli` npm package that resolves the right prebuilt binary at runtime via optionalDependencies, matching the esbuild/biome/oxc pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: New ts/ workspace with package.json declaring optionalDependencies: @talon/cli-darwin-arm64, -darwin-x64, -linux-x64, -linux-arm64, -win32-x64
ts/src/binary.ts: resolveBinary() walks optionalDependencies matching process.platform + process.arch, returns the binary path
ts/src/child.ts: mcpChildSpec(): { command, args: ['--mcp'], env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv } per design spec §9
ts/src/index.ts barrel export
Vitest tests for resolution logic with mocked platform/arch
npm pack produces a valid tarball; npm test clean

userStories.title: Cross-build per-platform binaries with cargo zigbuild
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want `cargo zigbuild` to produce stripped release binaries for darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-x64, linux-aarch64, win32-x64 so each npm subpackage ships a working binary.
acceptanceCriteria: Document zig + cargo-zigbuild install in DEVELOPMENT.md (or equivalent)

Build matrix script (justfile or shell) runs: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin / x86_64-apple-darwin / x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu / aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu / x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
Each target output is stripped and < 30 MB
Each binary runs --version on its target platform (CI matrix or qemu/wine smoke)
Per-platform npm subpackage layout: package.json + the prebuilt binary, no JS
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add release workflow and version tagging
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want a tag push to trigger the cross-build matrix and publish all platform packages plus the meta package to npm in one shot.
acceptanceCriteria: New .github/workflows/release.yml triggered by v* tag push
Matrix invokes US-021 build script, packages each platform binary, npm publish each subpackage and the meta package
Version is read from Cargo.toml; ts/package.json version stays in lockstep
Dry-run mode (workflow_dispatch with input) goes through every step except publish
Dry-run succeeds end-to-end on a clean checkout

userStories.title: Embed SKILL.md and wire --skill flag
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want `talon --skill` to print an embedded SKILL.md so agents can self-load Talon usage instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Author docs/SKILL.md (port the agent prompt from the TS reference)
include_str!("../../docs/SKILL.md") embeds it into the binary
--skill prints the contents and exits 0
Test: assert_cmd verifies non-empty output + exit 0
just verify passes

userStories.title: Polish human CLI output
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want terminal-width-aware wrapping, colored headings, compact result cards, and a spinner during search so the human output is pleasant.
acceptanceCriteria: Add terminal_size dep (full semver) if not present
Search results render as compact cards: path, score, snippet — wrapped to terminal width
Headings use existing anstyle/anstream styling
Spinner from existing spinner module wired for search command
Snapshot tests via insta (one snapshot per command's human output)
just verify passes

userStories.title: Add ranking regression test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want golden expected ranks for canonical queries so silent ranking drift is caught in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Port tests/services/talon/ranking-regression.test.ts to crates/talon-core/tests/ranking_regression.rs
Golden expected top-K paths for ~10 fixture queries
Score drift tolerated within 1e-3; rank order changes fail the test
Any TS-vs-Rust intentional divergence documented in a comment with reason
Suite green; just verify passes

userStories.title: Add CLI contract test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want assert_cmd-driven contract tests so the CLI's exit codes, help text, and JSON envelope shape are pinned and cannot regress silently.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/talon-cli/tests/cli.rs using assert_cmd + predicates
Covers: help text shape, exit code per error class, JSON envelope shape (action, ok, data/error fields) for every command
Ports relevant assertions from tests/services/talon/cli.test.ts and contract.test.ts
Suite green; just verify passes

userStories.title: Final verification: release build, parity, manual smokes
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want every shippable surface verified: release build, parity against TS for canonical queries, manual smoke of every command — before tagging v0.1.0.
acceptanceCriteria: just verify green
cargo build --release succeeds; stripped binary < 30 MB
talon --version, talon --help, talon --skill all work
talon init creates ~/.config/talon/config.toml from template
talon sync indexes a real vault end-to-end
talon embed populates vec_chunks against a live TEI sidecar (manual smoke; document command in NOTES.md)
talon search '<query>' returns real ranked results (manual smoke)
talon related <path>, talon meta --tag-counts, talon lint orphans all work on real data
talon watch reacts to fs events (manual smoke)
talon --mcp survives tools/list + tools/call from an MCP inspector or hand-crafted JSON-RPC (manual smoke)
Parity: 5+ canonical search queries return matching top-10 paths against TS reference (top-K order must match; score drift allowed)
v0.1.0 tag pushed; US-022 release workflow publishes successfully (dry-run first if uncertain)
